page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate solid multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 58,004.62 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,780.0, both placing in the average tier. Single-core performance is slightly weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1,086.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.63 GB/sec. However, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, scoring 21,336.19 in Passmark database tests. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 2,259.49 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 165.27 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The t2a-standard-32 is highly suited for scale-out workloads, static web serving, and database operations. However, it exhibits poor performance in large-scale LLM text generation, with the 70B model yielding only 2.31 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture offers a resource-dense, power-efficient alternative to traditional x86 instances for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and general-purpose containerized microservices.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by the 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. Equipped with 128 GB of RAM, it maintains a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 58,004.62 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,780.0. It excels in database operations, scoring 21,336.19 in Passmark database tests, and delivers 30.04 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and large LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. This instance is highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, database operations, and software compilation where ARM-based resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM. It delivers strong database performance and balanced multi-core efficiency for scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-32 is an ARM-based instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong database performance.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering balanced multi-core performance and strong database efficiency for scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Strong database performance with 21,336.19 Passmark database score
Average multi-core performance suitable for software compilation and web serving
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Optimized for ARM-native containerized applications and scale-out workloads
